summary: Added the Phase 2 PutBlob one-shot clients task brief.
Added Rust PutBlob frame encoding, relay command plumbing, RelayHandle one-shot upload API, and focused Rust tests.
Added TypeScript sendTypedRequest transport seam, putBlob helper, exports, and focused TypeScript tests.
Backfilled the Phase 2 coordination event log for existing PR commits.
Preserved request-type and put_blob timeout labels after sendTypedRequest refactor.

Phase 2 implementation matches the brief. CI green on PR #2742. RelayHandle::put_blob_one_shot, encode_frame, SendPutBlob command, sendTypedRequest split, putBlob TS helper, BlobUploadError class — all wired correctly. Three minor nits below; none block landing.

findings.finding: sendTypedRequest(FrameType.REQUEST, ...) calls requestHandler({id,payload,timeoutMs}) — a different argument shape than sendRequest's requestHandler(request, options). Existing tests don't exercise REQUEST through sendTypedRequest, so it's latent. Either route REQUEST back through sendRequest internally, or document the dual signature on requestHandler. Not blocking; ergonomic only.

PutBlobHeader::encode_frame returns Result<Vec<u8>, serde_json::Error>. serde_json serialization of this enum is total — the only failure path is OOM, which serde_json reports as Error. Could be infallible by encoding to a Vec<u8> in-memory writer, but the ? in put_blob_one_shot works fine via From<serde_json::Error> for SyncError. Cosmetic.

30s timeout duplicated as PUT_BLOB_TIMEOUT (Rust) and PUT_BLOB_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000 (TS). Acceptable since both are client-side and the daemon does not enforce. Flag for Phase 5 when more knobs may want central config.

Deliverable §4 (runtimed-client surface) was correctly skipped. RelayHandle is reachable via notebook-sync re-export; downstream Rust callers receive it directly. No wrapper needed. Confirming this is the intended call.

Brief checklist (encode_frame single-source, register-before-send, timeout eviction, BlobUpload typed error, sendTypedRequest interface, BlobUploadError class, no allow-list churn, full test coverage) — all green. CI passing across 24 jobs. Ready to mark non-draft.

summary: Preserved DirectTransport typed REQUEST handling by reconstructing the normal request handler shape and adding focused test coverage.
